channel.title: Parla Releases
channel.description: Sparkle update feed for Parla — local-first macOS dictation + meeting recorder.

item.title: Parla 2.28
item.description: v2.28 — fully-scrubbed binary. The cert real-name leak (1 occurrence of the developer's full name visible via 'strings Parla.app/Contents/MacOS/Parla') is now gone. Three changes: (1) ROTATED THE CODE-SIGNING CERT — the self-signed DictationLocalDev cert was regenerated with subject 'CN=DictationLocalDev / O=Parla Local Dev / OU=Parla / C=US' (no personal info). The previous cert with the developer's name has been deleted from the keychain. (2) PATCHED setup-dev-cert.sh to add 'security add-trusted-cert -p codeSign' as part of cert provisioning so future regenerations don't fall back to ad-hoc signing on the next build. (3) SCRUBBED the example text in Preferences → Cleanup → Custom Vocabulary. The placeholder previously read 'Example: Linus Bleinroth / Anthropic / Pyannote' — now generic ('Kubernetes / Postgres / Pyannote'). Final binary now contains zero occurrences of either the developer's home-folder path OR full name. ⚠️ IMPORTANT for users updating from v2.27 or earlier: the new code signature is different, so macOS TCC will treat Parla as a 'new app' on first launch after the update. You'll be re-prompted to grant Microphone, Accessibility, and Input Monitoring permissions ONCE — this only happens this one time. Going forward, signature is stable and grants persist across rebuilds.

item.title: Parla 2.27
item.description: v2.27 — three additive improvements. (1) DEFENSIVE EMAIL HEADER SCRUB: post-cleanup regex strips any leading 'To:/Subject:/From:/Cc:/Bcc:/Date:/Reply-To:/Sender:' lines that Gemma might still hallucinate despite v2.26's prompt fix. Belt-and-suspenders. (2) CALENDAR PRE-ARM: scans the user's calendars every 60s for events starting within 2 minutes whose body/location/url contains a meeting URL (Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, Webex, Whereby, Around). Fires the same record-meeting prompt before the user even joins. Gated on calendarIntegrationEnabled and full-access calendar permission. De-duped per event. (3) PHASE 4 — COREAUDIO PER-PROCESS ATTRIBUTION: closes the v2.25 auto-stop edge case where Zoom/Teams stays running after a meeting ends. SystemAudioWatcher uses kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList + kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunningInput (macOS 14.4+) to enumerate which apps are using the mic, filters out our own PID, and triggers the auto-stop after 30s of zero non-Parla mic activity. Deployment target bumped from 14.0 to 14.4 to enable the per-process API.

item.title: Parla 2.26
item.description: v2.26 — fixes the email-headers-being-hallucinated bug. When dictating into Mail.app / Outlook / Gmail (or anywhere else the bundle-ID classifier flagged .email), Gemma was inserting fake headers like 'To: User Subject: Speech Recognition Issue Hi, …' in front of the body. Two compounding issues: (1) the FORMAT DETECTION block in the cleanup prompt had a To:/Subject:/greeting/closing TEMPLATE that the model latched onto. (2) v2.22's bundle-ID classifier made .email fire much more often (any time you're in Mail.app), so the over-application became visible. Fixed by rewriting both the FORMAT DETECTION email rules AND the contextInstruction(.email) hint to say 'body only — never output header lines under any circumstances'. Matches how Wispr Flow / Aqua Voice handle the same case (the user is already in their email client; cleanup produces body text only).

item.title: Parla 2.25
item.description: v2.25 — Phase 3: auto-stop meeting recording when the meeting ends. Two trigger sources work in parallel: (1) the browser tab leaves the meeting URL (BrowserMeetingWatcher.onMeetingURLEnded) — covers Google Meet / Zoom-in-browser / Teams-in-browser / Webex / Whereby / Around / Discord; (2) a native meeting app (Zoom.app, Teams.app, Webex Meetings.app, Discord.app, Slack.app) terminates — observed via NSWorkspace.didTerminateApplicationNotification. Either signal calls StatusBarController.autoStopMeetingRecording() which wraps finishMeetingRecording with a labeled trigger reason. New user toggle: Preferences → Meetings → Auto-stop → 'Auto-stop recording when meeting ends' (default ON). The menu bar Stop button still works for manual stop at any time. Edge case not covered yet: native meeting apps that stay running after a meeting ends (Zoom in particular often does this) — those still require manual stop. Phase 4 if needed: CoreAudio per-process attribution to detect when other-than-Parla processes stop using mic.

item.title: Parla 2.24
item.description: v2.24 — Phase 2: browser-hosted meeting detection. The existing native meeting-app detector (Zoom.app, Teams.app, Webex.app, Discord, Slack) now has a companion that polls running browsers' active tab URLs every 3 seconds and fires the SAME 'Record this meeting?' prompt when it finds a meeting URL. Detected URL patterns: meet.google.com/<3-4-3 letter code>, zoom.us/(j|wc|w)/, teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join, teams.live.com/meet, *.webex.com/(meet|join), whereby.com/, around.co/r/, discord.com/channels/.... Browsers covered: Chrome (+canary +beta), Safari (+TP), Arc, Brave, Edge, Vivaldi, Opera. Firefox excluded — doesn't expose URL via AppleScript. Each meeting URL only fires once; repeated polls don't re-prompt. Gated on the same calendarIntegrationEnabled flag as the native detector.

item.title: Parla 2.23
item.description: v2.23 — privacy + bug fixes. (1) Public DMG no longer leaks the developer's home-folder path via MLX C++ debug strings. The 25 `/Users//...` strings that were visible to anyone running `strings Parla` are now scrubbed via a post-build pass (build.sh → scripts/scrub-binary.py). The cert-embedded developer name will be cleaned in a later opt-in release (regenerating the signing cert requires re-granting macOS TCC permissions, so it's a deliberate user choice). (2) Settings → Help → 'Open Meeting Browser' button now actually opens the browser. Same root cause as the Sparkle bug we fixed in v2.20: `NSApp.delegate as? AppDelegate` returns nil under SwiftUI's @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor in Settings-only scenes. Routed through a new StatusBarController.shared singleton (matching the SparkleUpdater.shared pattern). The 'Open Welcome' button in the same panel got the same fix.
